page: Amazon Web Services c7g.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton3 processors using the ARM64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz based on the Neoverse-V1 ARMv8 design. The processor allocates 1.0 thread per core, eliminating simultaneous multithreading overhead. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and does not include a GPU. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory throughput. The server achieves a strong rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core testing. Memory bandwidth is a notable highlight, with Passmark memory scores reaching the top-tier category and read-only small block cached memory bandwidth measured at 4652.28 GB/sec. Large block uncached read and write bandwidths are also strong at 271.87 GB/sec and 116.04 GB/sec respectively. In application-specific benchmarks, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, database operations, and static web serving throughput. Redis SET operations reach 76.41 million operations per second.

The c7g.12xlarge offers high resource density and compute efficiency due to its dedicated physical cores and ARM-based architecture, which typically delivers high performance-per-resource compared to traditional x86 architectures. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and the absence of x86 instruction sets means workloads must be compatible with ARM64. This server is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, high-performance web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded scientific simulations.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the ARM64 AWS Graviton processor family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz on the Neoverse-V1 architecture, paired with 96.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or a GPU, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier memory bandwidth, with read-only cached memory bandwidth reaching 4652.28 GB/sec. The server excels in multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, and database operations. It offers high resource density and compute efficiency for ARM-compatible workloads, though it lacks local storage and x86 vector instructions. This server is best suited for high-throughput web serving, distributed databases, and compute-heavy engineering workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 48 dedicated cores, 96 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and top-tier memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.12xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server featuring 48 dedicated physical cores, 96 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance delivering dedicated physical cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated physical cores on ARM64 Neoverse-V1 architecture
96.0 GB of system memory with top-tier memory bandwidth performance
22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core CPU, database, and static web serving benchmarks
No local storage or GPU, requiring network-attached storage
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and database workloads
